name: proxelar
description: A programmable MITM proxy that intercepts HTTP/HTTPS traffic so you don't have to guess what your app is    doing. Forward & reverse modes, TLS interception, TUI, terminal, and web GUI.
readme_summary: Proxelar is a Man-in-the-Middle proxy written in Rust, designed to intercept, inspect, and modify HTTP/HTTPS traffic. It offers flexible interfaces including a TUI and a web GUI, and supports Lua scripting for runtime traffic manipulation.
key_features: Lua scripting for request/response modification, blocking, or mocking
Automatic CA generation for HTTPS interception
Forward and reverse proxy modes
Multiple interfaces: Terminal, TUI (ratatui), and Web GUI (axum + WebSocket)
Request filtering and detailed inspection
Easy CA installation via a dedicated local domain
primary_use_case: Intercepting, inspecting, and modifying HTTP/HTTPS traffic for debugging, reverse engineering, testing, and automation.
cover_image_prompt: A wise old librarian sits at a grand oak desk, meticulously examining a stream of glowing digital letters flowing from a pipe labeled 'Internet' into a complex network of gears and levers. The librarian uses a magnifying glass to scrutinize individual letters, sometimes rerouting them with a flick of their wrist or changing their color. Some letters are caught in a 'block' bin, while others are transformed and sent down a different pipe labeled 'App'. Subtle UI elements showing code snippets and traffic logs are visible on a nearby screen. The overall mood is one of focused control and understanding. The art style is a 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette and clear, defined elements, emphasizing the 'inspection and modification' aspect of network traffic.

name: uncover
description: Quickly discover exposed hosts on the internet using multiple search engines.
readme_summary: Uncover is a Go-based command-line tool that leverages multiple search engine APIs to discover publicly exposed hosts on the internet. It's designed for automation and integration into existing security and reconnaissance workflows.
key_features: Query multiple search engines simultaneously
Supports Shodan, Censys, FOFA, Hunter, Quake, ZoomEye, Netlas, CriminalIP, PublicWWW, HunterHow, Google, Onyphe, and Driftnet
Multiple API key input and automatic randomization
stdin/stdout support for input and output
Built with automation in mind for pipeline integration
primary_use_case: Discovering exposed hosts on the internet by querying multiple search engines through a unified CLI interface.
cover_image_prompt: A digital cartographer meticulously drawing a vast, interconnected network map on a glowing holographic globe. The cartographer uses a magnifying glass that reveals hidden islands (exposed hosts) on the globe, each emitting a faint digital signal. Small, stylized icons representing different search engines (like Shodan, Censys, Google) are subtly integrated into the cartographer's tools and the map's legend. The scene is set in a futuristic, dimly lit observatory, with data streams flowing around the globe. The overall mood is one of discovery and intelligence gathering. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style with a dark, tech-focused color palette and glowing elements.

name: reactos
readme_summary: ReactOS is an open-source operating system aiming for compatibility with applications and drivers designed for Microsoft Windows NT family. It is currently in alpha development, meaning it is unstable and best tested in virtual environments.
key_features: Windows NT family compatibility (applications and drivers)
Open Source development
Alpha quality, under heavy development
Build environment (RosBE) for Windows and Unix/GNU-Linux
Support for building bootable CD images
Installation on FAT16/FAT32 partitions
primary_use_case: To develop a free, open-source operating system that can run Windows applications and drivers.
cover_image_prompt: A determined architect meticulously building a bridge labeled 'ReactOS' that connects a vibrant, modern city (representing Windows applications and drivers) to a vast, unexplored landscape. The architect is using blueprints that show lines of code and operating system symbols. Small, glowing digital particles representing compatibility flow from the architect's tools onto the bridge. In the background, a faint silhouette of the Windows logo can be seen, emphasizing the goal of compatibility. The scene is bright and hopeful, with a clean, 3D isometric illustration style, highlighting the construction and connection aspects.

name: mujoco
description: Multi-Joint dynamics with Contact. A general purpose physics simulator.
readme_summary: MuJoCo is a high-performance physics engine designed for simulating articulated structures with contact. It is optimized for speed and accuracy, making it suitable for research in robotics, biomechanics, graphics, and machine learning.
key_features: C API for low-level control and performance
Interactive visualization with native GUI (OpenGL)
XML compiler for preallocating data structures
Python bindings for ease of use
Unity game engine plug-in
MJX (MuJoCo XLA) for JAX integration
primary_use_case: Accurate and fast simulation of articulated structures interacting with their environment, primarily for research and development in robotics, biomechanics, and machine learning.
cover_image_prompt: A master craftsman meticulously assembling a complex clockwork automaton, with intricate gears and joints moving in perfect harmony. The automaton is interacting with a miniature environment, demonstrating dynamic balance and contact. Subtle holographic blueprints and glowing data streams flow around the craftsman, representing the underlying physics calculations. The scene is set in a well-lit workshop with a clean, modern aesthetic. The overall mood is one of precision and innovation. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing the detailed mechanics and the seamless integration of simulation and real-world interaction, with the name 'MuJoCo' subtly etched onto a tool.
